# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
    DOCKER_DRIVER="overlay2" # Or "systemd". Defaults to systemd on Unix systems, overlay on Windows as an alternative. Can be set in CI/CD settings for all jobs or specified job by setting `DOCKER_DRIVER` variable per stage
    DOCKER_HOST: 'tcp://docker:2375' # defaults to the Docker daemon at localhost:2375 on Unix systems, can be set in CI/CD settings for all jobs or specified job by setting `DOCKER_DRIVER` variable per stage
    GITLAB_TOKEN: 'your-gitlab-ci-token' # your GitLab private token with appropriate permissions to access the repo and create pipelines. Set in CI/CD settings for all jobs or specified job by setting `GITLAB_TOKEN` variable per stage
    NEW_RELEASES_CHATWORK: 'new releases are here : https://chatwork.com' # Replace this with your ChatWork room ID to receive new release notifications in webhook format, can be set on GitLab CI/CD settings for all jobs or specified job by setting `NEW_RELEASES_CHATWORK` variable per stage
    REPO: 'gitlab-org/gitlab' # Repo that contains the project. Defaults to gitlab org and repo if not specificed in a pipeline but set as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, can be overwritten by setting `REPO` variable per job
    SAFE_PATH: '/home/.ssh/id_rsa' # Private key path. Defaults to /tmp/keyfile if not specified on the .gitlab-ci file or runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, can be set in a pipeline by setting `SAFE_PATH` variable per job
    SONAR_TOKEN: 'yoursonarQubeToken' # Sonarqube token with appropriate permissions to analyze source code. Set only for sonar analysis and not as part of .gitlab-ci file or runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, can be set in a pipeline by setting `SONAR_TOKEN` variable per job
    IMAGE: 'node' # Docker image that will create the release. Defaults to node for latest stable tag from your container registry/docker hub etc based on REPO value if not specified or overridden at .gitlab-ci file level, can be set in a pipeline by setting `IMAGE` variable per job
    GIT_BRANCH: 'master' # Git branch to build. Defaults master for latest stable tag from your container registry/docker hub etc based on REPO value if not specified or overridden at .gitlab-ci file level, can be set in a pipeline by setting `GIT_BRANCH` variable per job
    GIFT: 'https://gift.yourwebsite' # Defaults to https://giftracer.com but should point your app on GiftTrigger API for deploying new releases from CI/CD pipelines, can be set in a pipeline by setting `GIFT` variable per job
    CHATWORK_URL: 'https://chatwork.com' # ChatWork room URL to receive notifications about MRs merged and the release notes are sent there via webhook format on Merge Request Web Hook Events, can be set in a pipeline by setting `CHATWORK_ROOM` variable per job
    NEW_RELEASES: 'true'  # Set this as true or false depending if you want to create new releases. If it is not specified then default value of the boolean should also work like so, can be set in a pipeline by setting `NEW_RELEASES` at global level and per job
    REGISTRY: 'docker-registry' # Defaults docker registry but needs your .gitlab-ci file to specify it if you want Docker Hub or any other private container repository. Can be set in a pipeline by setting `REGISTRY_URL` variable for each project, can also default as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section
    REGISTRY_USER: 'docker-registry' # Defaults to docker/docker registry but needs your .gitlab-ci file or the job setting. Can be set in a pipeline by using `REGISTRY` variable for each project, can also default as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section
    REGISTRY_PASSWORD: 'yourDockerRegistryPassword' # Defaults to docker/docker registry but needs your .gitlab-ci file or the job setting. Can be set in a pipeline by using `REGISTRY` variable for each project, can also default as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section
    DEPLOY_STAGE: 'prod' # Stage where application to deploy on deployment server defaults to prod but needs .gitlab-ci file setting. Can be set in a pipeline by using `DEPLOY` variable per job if you want any other stage else use default value from the script
    DEPLOY_SERVER: "http://deployserver" # Defaults as http://localhost where your application is deployed, but needs .gitlab-ci file setting. Can be set in a pipeline by using `DEPLOY` variable per job if you want any other server else use default value from the script
    TIMEOUT: '3m'  # Timeout duration for deployment stage Defaults to minute and can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    NOTIFY: 'false'  # Flag if you want notification after the deployment Defaults to false but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    SONAR_RUN: 'false' # Flag if you want analysis after deployment Defaults to false but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    SONAR_REPO: 'sonar' # Repository for sonar analysis Defaults to user/project but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    SONAR_BRANCH: 'master' # Branch for sonar analysis Defaults to master but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    RELEASE_NAME: ''  # Release name is auto generated based on branch and merge request title Defaults to '', but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    NOTES: 'false'  # If true generates auto release notes if false will skip generating Defaults to FALSE but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    RELEASE_NOTES: 'true'  # If true auto generates release notes if false will skip generating Defaults to TRUE but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    DOCKER_DRIVER: 'systemd'  # Docker driver Defaults to systemd on Unix systems and overlay2 for Windows as an alternative but can override within runner settings GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    DOCKER_HOST: 'tcp://docker'  # Docker host Defaults to localhost on Unix systems and tcp:/localhost for Windows as an alternative but can override within runner settings GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    NEW_RELEASES: 'false'  # If false do not generate new releases Defaults to FALSE but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    REGISTRY: 'registry'  # Registries name Defaults to registry but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    REGISTRY_USER: 'registryUser'  # Registries user Defaults to registry but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    REGISTRY_PASSWORD: 'registryPassword'  # Registries password Defaults to registry but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    DEPLOY_STAGE: 'prod'  # Deploy stages Defaults to prod but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    DEPLOY_SERVER: 'http://deploymentServer'  # Deploy server Defaults to http//localhost where your application is deployed but can override as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    TIMEOUT: '3m'  # Timeouts Defaults to minute and overridden if provided else as part of runner settings within GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    NOTIFY: 'false'  # Notifies Defaults to false but can override as mentioned below on `GITLAB_NOTIFIER` within runner settings GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
    NOTIFY: 'true'  # Notifies Defaults to true but can override as mentioned below on `GITLAB_NOTIFIER` within runner settings GitLab CI environment variables section, when not specified then it should be same by .gitlab-ci file level. Can set in a pipeline or job setting at specific time
